item.title: Polish Arms Maker Set for Record Sales on Defense Spending Spree
item.description: Poland’s biggest defense group is targeting another record year as the European Union’s largest land force ramps up spending to counter threats from Russia and tap into new EU funding.

item.title: Russia Set to Ramp Up Oil Flows as Impact of Drone Strikes Fades
item.description: Moscow has resumed loading operations at key ports, setting the stage for a rebound in exports

item.title: AI is Already Finding Thousands of Software Flaws, Even Before Anthropic’s Mythos
item.description: A bug-tracking program found 200 serious issues in roughly a week.

item.title: Ireland Trims Economic-Growth Projections on Iran Conflict
item.description: Ireland cut this year’s economic-growth projection, citing the conflict in the Middle East.

item.title: Switch Lands $2.6 Billion of Bank Pledges to Power Data Centers
item.description: Switch Inc. gathered $2.6 billion of bank pledges to help the data-center operator procure electricity, a mounting challenge for the booming industry and one of its biggest costs.

item.title: Fed Chair Nominee Kevin Warsh to Testify at Confirmation Hearing
item.description: Kevin Warsh Photographer: Tierney L. Cross/Bloomberg

item.title: Flurry of US IPOs Race to Tap Market Ahead of SpaceX Debut
item.description: The US IPO market has gone from desolate to bustling in a matter of weeks, with companies looking to raise as much as $17.3 billion this month alone and capitalize on stocks’ resilience.

item.title: US Retail Sales Surge by Most in a Year as Spending Extends Beyond Gas
item.description: US retail sales soared in March by the most in a year, suggesting consumers continued to spend on a wide array of merchandise despite a surge in gasoline prices sparked by the Iran war.

item.title: SEC Monitoring ‘Emerging Pressures’ in Private Credit Space
item.description: The Securities and Exchange Commission is closely monitoring the “emerging pressures” in the private credit market as redemption requests persist amid rising default-rate projections, Chairman Paul Atkins said in prepared remarks.

item.title: Lira Swaps Signal Turkey Will Hold Off Hiking Rates This Meeting
item.description: Swaps tied to the Turkish lira are signaling that traders have pared bets for the central bank to raise interest rates, after the ceasefire in the Iran war pushed oil prices off their highs and eased inflation fears.

item.title: Vanke Wins Backing for Bond Delay Plan, Easing Default Risk
item.description: China Vanke Co. won enough creditor backing to extend payments on a yuan bond due Thursday for a year, easing the embattled developer’s risk of an imminent default, at least for now.

item.title: Baird Capital Raises $450 Million for Third Private Equity Fund
item.description: The private equity arm of financial services firm Robert W. Baird & Co. has raised $450 million for a fund that will invest in companies in the US and the UK.

item.title: US Premarket Movers for April 21, 2026
item.description: S&P 500 Index futures rise 0.5% as of 7:50 a.m. in New York as investors wait to learn whether Iran will join key peace talks.

item.title: Meloni Demands Terna CEO Choose: €7.3 Million Payout or Eni Role
item.description: Italian Premier Giorgia Meloni said the outgoing chief executive officer of grid operator Terna SpA, Giuseppina di Foggia, should choose between a €7.3 million ($8.6 million) severance or a new role as chairwoman of ENI SpA, making clear the government’s stance that she can’t have both.

item.title: Top Oil Traders Warn Worst of Demand Hit From War Is Yet to Come
item.description: The world’s top oil traders said that demand destruction wrought by the Iran war is set to deepen, a sign the full economic impact of the conflict may be yet to come.

item.title: S&P 500 Futures Rally as Traders Await Updates on Iran, Warsh
item.description: US stock-index futures rose before the bell on Tuesday as traders awaited developments on Iran and Kevin Warsh’s Senate hearing amid the latest round of corporate earnings.

item.title: Hybrid Bond Buybacks Return After War Paused Riskier Debt Deals
item.description: European companies are once again buying back junior bonds after the market turmoil sparked by the war in the Middle East and the AI-related software selloff put some riskier debt deals on ice.

item.title: Spain Air Passenger Growth in First Quarter Slows From Year Ago
item.description: Spain’s airports handled 24.76 million passengers in March 2026, up 3.9% from a year earlier, according to provisional data from Aena published Monday.

item.title: Moody’s Lifts Thai Outlook to Stable on Easing Tariff Shocks
item.description: Moody’s Ratings upgraded Thailand’s credit outlook to stable from negative, citing easing downside risks from US tariffs and improved domestic investment momentum. The sovereign rating was affirmed at Baa1.

item.title: Higher Oil Prices Pose a Dilemma for US Shale
item.description: Trump wants producers to unleash output, but many remember the ill-fated dash for growth more than a decade ago.

item.title: Davidson Kempner Ex-Partner Says He Blew Whistle, Was Forced Out
item.description: A former top trader and partner at Davidson Kempner Capital Management accused the firm and its investing chief, Tony Yoseloff, of ousting him last year after he attempted to expose wrongdoing.

item.title: Equifax Posts Fastest Revenue Growth Since 2021 on Mortgages
item.description: Equifax Inc. reported its fastest revenue growth in more than four years, driven by a surge in mortgage applications.

item.title: Exports From South Korea and Taiwan Surge Amid Demand for AI Gear
item.description: Korea’s chip exports jumped 183%, showing resilience amid energy supply headwinds

item.title: Magnum Ice Cream Ranks Among Biggest Short Seller Bets in Europe
item.description: Magnum Ice Cream Co., the maker of Ben & Jerry’s, Breyers and Klondike bars, has become one of the most popular short bets in Europe as it grapples with shrinking profits and changing consumer appetites.

item.title: China’s Proya Cosmetics Earnings Drop as Core Brand Sales Weaken
item.description: Chinese beauty products maker Proya Cosmetics Co. reported 2025 revenue that declined year‑on‑year and missed analyst estimates, a trend that extended into the first quarter as sales of its core brand lost momentum.

item.title: Cook’s Apple Career Offers Wisdom on How to Deal with China
item.description: The outgoing CEO developed a playbook for success in the world’s No. 2 economy

item.title: BofA’s Doan Says Hungarian Bonds to Rally on Bid to Join Euro
item.description: The incoming Hungarian government is quickly taking its first steps to prepare the country to join the euro region, boding well for prolonging its steep bond rally, according a Bank of America Corp. economist.

item.title: Vitol Performed Well Despite ‘Triage’ Early in War, CEO Says
item.description: Vitol Group’s performance has been “good” so far this year in spite of a hectic period in the early days of the Iran war, according to Chief Executive Officer Russell Hardy.

item.title: UBS Set for Long-Awaited Clarity on Switzerland’s Capital Rules
item.description: UBS Group AG is finally about to find out just how many billions of dollars Switzerland will strip from its capital buffers.

item.title: Will Mythos Ruin or Save the Global Financial System?
item.description: Regulators and banks face a double-edged breakthrough that could destabilize—or secure—the global financial system.
